reviews.review_msg: Dr. Jaumouille is a great instructor who cares about student learning and student feedback. MBB 420 (Cancer Immunology) was being offered for the first time this semester, so it was a little rocky to begin with but eventually got on track. Dr. J's lectures are engaging and he is well spoken. He answers questions thoroughly and is very accessible.

I took MBB420 (Special Topics in Biochemistry) with Dr. Paetzel and thoroughly enjoyed it. He was clear about expectations, and the concepts were well explained. Exams and grading were very fair. It's always great to learn from someone passionate about the subject. I highly recommend this course.

I took MBB420 as an intersession class (2 months) with Dr. Sen. It was awesome! He's such a wise man and often coloured the class with interesting anecdotes about the people who discovered whatever we were studying. He was so chill. Really easy exams, too. Majority of the mark was class presentation and participation (60% together).

Dr. Holt is very knowledgable in his field and has incredibly interesting research, but his lectures are a little dry. He has very high expectations for the students in his classes, and is a relatively fair marker. The lectures are not recorded to encourage students to attend lecture, and he has a long list of questions to ask during presentations.

First time teaching at sfu, her exams are like first year, lots of writing but straightforward easy questions.  She tends to reuse some of the practice exam questions.  Her strong russian accent is very hard to understand at first but you will get used to it. Studying for this course heavily relied on the sub-notes in the slides. Marking is fair.

lectures were great. we had to hand in HW every week, but they were very easy. The Avg for the final was a little low. Her notes are very complete u don't really need to read the textbook Very interesting course

MBB 420 Microbial Pathogenesis was a great course. I found Dr. Craig's section of the course easy to follow, and her quizzes are fair.  She does speak a bit fast, but if you ask, she will slow down and repeat things.  I like her emphasis on getting us to know the basic facts.

Great lecturer, very interested in material and knows what she is talking about.  Notes provided are a little too detailed, but the full notes are always given out.  She was helpful, friendly, and her overall attitude towards students was great.  She lectures a bit fast at times, but will slow down if/when asked to.
One crazy lady.  She rode into class on the back of one of her dogs.  Good prof, but a bit rude
A really kind heart. He goes above and beyond to help students with assignments. A genuine teacher that wants the best for this students
